title: Obo Rin
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Obo}}
{{Character
|type=Galactic Empire

{{Quote|It is both troubling and exciting as a scientist that there is such a lack of standardization or base of knowledge in this field. It does mean that there is ample room for discovery, and many rumors and myths to dispel.|Obo Rin in a report to Major Vontenn|<ref name="SS" />}}
'''Obo Rin''' was a controversial [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[sentientologist]] and [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologist]] who worked for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Under commission by [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Pandur]] on behalf of the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] personal servant, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], Rin wrote the ''[[Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy]]'' as a reference for other scholars and Imperial personnel. Rin's correspondence with Pandur, Vader, and [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Vontenn]], the [[Imperial Liaison Officer of Sentientology Studies]], revealed him to be an Imperial loyalist and obsequious personality. However, Rin failed to endear himself to Major [[Herrit]], an [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] who was also involved in the project. This enmity was at least in part due to Rin's extravagant research expenses—totaling nearly one million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] during production of the first installment of the ''Catalog''. Despite an attempt by Herrit to discredit Rin and expose what Herrit alleged was a falsified ''curriculum vitae'', Vader, through his [[Aide/Legends|aide]] [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Solistein]], insisted that Rin continue his work and that Herrit assist him by obtaining a [[Duinuogwuin/Legends|Duinuogwuin]] corpse for dissection. Herrit [[Death/Legends|died]] during the mission, and Rin went on to produce a revised edition and further updates to the work without interference as a member of Vader's personal staff. He remained active, affiliating himself with the [[Imperial Remnant|remnants]] of the Empire after the defeat of [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and the rise of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

Rin's research drew on previous publications, classified data, personal observations, interviews, and dissections of the [[Species/Legends|species]] under study. However, Rin's pro-Imperial leanings colored his work, turning it into pure [[Propaganda/Legends|propaganda]] at [[Time/Legends|times]]. For instance, his ''Catalog'' claimed that the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] species had no contact with the outside [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] until Imperial [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] visited [[Dac/Legends|their world]], and he described several species that had been [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] by the Empire as willing laborers toiling for the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. Rin was a proponent of the theory that for a species to be considered sentient it had to be able not just to ponder abstract concepts but also to communicate such abstraction to others. He proposed a [[universal definition of life]] by which sentientologists could distinguish living species from other entities, such as [[Droid/Legends|droids]], and he rejected the likelihood of the evolution of [[Silicon/Legends|silicon]]-based life. Rin held that a species had to be understood in the context of its environment, and his ''Catalog'' included detailed overviews of the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworlds]] of the lifeforms discussed. Despite his espoused loyalties to the New Order, Rin occasionally expressed sympathy for species he deemed to have been unfairly stereotyped in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].

==Biography==
===Academic background===
{{Quote|Concerning Obo Rin's qualifications—Lord Vader did not order you to perform a background check. (FYI: Lord Vader has visited Sab Rufo.)|Captain Solistein, replying to a communiqué from Major Herrit questioning Obo Rin's academic background|<ref name="GG4a" />}}
Obo Rin was a [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="SS" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]] trained as both a [[sentientologist]]<ref name="GG4a" /> and a [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologist]].<ref name="GATORW">''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''</ref> His ''curriculum vitae'' showed that he had obtained two [[Doctor/Legends|doctoral]] degrees from the [[Academy at Sab Rufo]],<ref name="GG4a" /> an institution located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Sab Rufo]] in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}.</ref> The document also noted that during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Rin served as that institution's [[Director of Sentientology]]. Rin claimed to have conducted extensive postdoctoral research. For example, his credentials showed that he had studied at the [[Three Rings University of Social Sciences]] under the tutelage of the famed sentientologist [[Ditwar Logas]], and that he had earned further degrees from the [[Maxet Institute of Technology]].<ref name="GG4a" />

===''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy''===
{{Quote|Following this brief note is my first installment of the ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy''. It is difficult to express my delight in the fact that Lord Vader found my modest proposal to be of some small use to the Empire. Enclosed are voucher chips for my expenses. Please have reimbursement transferred to my personal account in the Imperial Bank of Korfo II.|Obo Rin, "Prologue," ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy''|<ref name="GG4a" />}}
[[File:Char-stub.png|thumb|left|250px|Rin wrote his ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'' at the behest of Darth Vader (pictured).]]
During the Galactic Civil War, Obo Rin sent a proposal to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to undertake a major research project: a work that would describe in detail the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], took a personal interest in the project, and Rin soon received a commission from the galactic government to undertake his proposed research. The assignment came via [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Pandur]], who was serving as Imperial [[Imperial Liaison|Liaison Officer]] of [[Korfo II/Legends|Korfo II]],<ref name="GG4a" /> a planet in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEAOC" />

With this approval, Rin began his investigations. He based his findings on personal observations, interviews with members of the species under consideration, and dissections of [[Death/Legends|cadavers]]. The sole exception was his research on the [[Charon/Legends|Charon]]—a species that inhabited a pocket dimension known as [[Otherspace]]; for information on the Charon, Rin relied on an inebriated [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] for whom Rin had bought drinks in a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] on the planet [[Duron]]. Rin's subsequent reports had to be sent through the appropriate channels by way of Pandur. Another administrative contact, [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Herrit]] of [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]], took charge of allocating funds, personnel, and other materials to the sentientologist.<ref name="GG4a" /> Rin's work also fell within the purview of the [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|Commission for the Preservation of the New Order]] (COMPNOR).<ref name="SMUIU">{{JournalCite|15|Special Military Unit Intelligence Update}}</ref>

Rin eventually completed the first installment of what he envisioned to be a much greater work, entitled the ''[[Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy]]''. In the draft manuscript, Rin included a short introduction outlining his theoretical approach to the definitions of life and sentience, and focused entries on those species he deemed to be of particular interest to the Empire—whether as potential threats or potential opportunities. He included information on each species's physical description, [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], temperament, culture, and a representative quote. Rin also provided a numerical representation of each species's relative abilities in various aptitudes. In total, Rin covered forty-four species, ranging from the [[Abyssin/Legends|Abyssin]] to the [[Xi'Dec/Legends|Xi'Dec]]. Rin also noted that his entry on the Charon, although based on hearsay, was included so that Darth Vader could analyze it and come to his own conclusions as to its degree of reliability.<ref name="GG4a" />

Rin sent the text to Lieutenant Pandur and the [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]] vouchers for reimbursement to Major Herrit. His expense report—including requests for 10,000 credits for a night's stay on [[Barab I/Legends|Barab I]], 37,200 credits for the chance to speak with a [[Duinuogwuin/Legends|Duinuogwuin]], and 152,317 credits for transportation to the [[Kaelta system]]—totaled almost a million credits. Rin also requested an escort of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to aid him in obtaining specimens for dissection. The [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] indicated that the findings were still in their preliminary stages and that he anticipated that continued work on the greater project would keep him occupied for several [[Galactic Standard Calendar|standard years]].<ref name="GG4a" />

===Reception===
{{Quote|In conclusion, as it is apparent to me that Obo Rin hopes to bilk the Empire out of millions of credits, I must again request that you allow the Imperial Intelligence to handle this survey.|Major Herrit in a letter to Darth Vader|<ref name="GG4a" />}}
[[File:Duinuogwuin-GatORW.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Rin's rival, Major Herrit, died trying to procure a Duinuogwuin cadaver for the scholar.]]
The first installment of Rin's work was received favorably by Pandur and Vader.<ref name="GG4b">[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races (Second Edition)|''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races'', Second Edition]]</ref> Herrit, on the other hand, contacted Vader to protest Rin's writings, questioning the veracity of both the document and of Rin's claimed academic background. Herrit averred that not only was the Academy at Sab Rufo non-existent, but that there was no such place as Sab Rufo at all. He dismissed the Three Rings University of Social Sciences as "a [[Computer/Legends|compu]]-order diploma house," and presented the Maxet Institute of Technology as a trade school that lacked the accreditation to bestow upon Rin the degrees the sentientologist claimed to have earned there. Herrit also took issue with Rin's expenditures, holding that the claimed sums were exorbitant. Accordingly, the major ordered the [[Office of Budgetary Control]] to deny Rin's request for reimbursement. In conclusion, Herrit held that Imperial Intelligence was better positioned to compile a work about sentient species than Rin could ever be. The [[Officer/Legends|officer]] thus urged Darth Vader to reconsider any further assignments for the scientist and to hand the project over to Imperial Intelligence.<ref name="GG4a" />

Darth Vader replied via a communiqué from his personal [[Aide/Legends|aide]], [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Solistein]]. The message expressed the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord's]] disapproval of Herrit's presumptuous recommendations and the unrequested background check on Rin—Solistein further revealed that Vader had himself visited Sab Rufo and knew it to be a real place. The Sith Lord commanded Herrit to rescind his order to deny Rin's reimbursement requests, and specified that the sentientologist be allowed to continue his work unhindered—with the stormtrooper escort he had requested. Solistein's message further conveyed Vader's order that Major Herrit personally locate and provide a Duinuogwuin cadaver for Rin to dissect.<ref name="GG4a" /> Herrit died trying to fulfill this command.<ref name="GG4b" />

===Revised edition===
{{Quote|I have greatly appreciated the response of both Lord Vader and Lieutenant Pandur to my first edition. The prestige afforded by my contract with the Empire has enabled me to call upon individuals whom I would otherwise have been unable to consult, greatly increasing the validity of the information contained within this work.|Obo Rin in a letter to Major Vontenn|<ref name="GG4b" />}}
Vader's patronage and Rin's position as the preeminent Imperial sentientologist allowed him to push his research to greater levels as he moved beyond his first draft of the ''Catalog''. Rin also gained access to high-level [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] and [[Noble/Legends|dignitaries]], who provided him with even the most difficult-to-find data.<ref name="GG4b" />

Another Imperial functionary, Major [[Vontenn]], who served as the [[Imperial Liaison Officer of Sentientology Studies]], became involved in the project. Vontenn sent requests for changes and updates as well as corrections of the work Rin had already submitted. Rin eventually sent a revised version of his first installment of the ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', covering the same species as the previous edition. The work's purview changed slightly; in the revised version, Rin claimed that the species covered represented "the most important sentient species in the galaxy." He also indicated that the document was intended for use by all sentientologists authorized by the Empire. The volume maintained the introduction from the first edition, but it introduced a new format for the species covered, partitioning information into regularized sections for each species while also discontinuing the representative quotes. In the revised draft, Rin admitted that his information on the [[Sedrian]] species was based on his personal theories, [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] tales, and intercepted communications from members of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. In his cover letter to Vontenn, Rin conveyed his sympathies to Herrit's family for the major's death.<ref name="GG4b" />

Rin continued to add information to the ''Catalog''. For example, he compiled reports on the [[Ailon (species)/Legends|Ailon]],<ref name="SMUIU" /> the [[Yevetha]],<ref name="CTD">''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]''</ref> and at least one species with the ability to dramatically [[Changeling/Legends|alter its shape]]—a trait exhibited by the [[Ugor/Legends|Ugors]], covered in his first installment of the work.<ref name="SS" /> He added information to the ''Catalog'' to help [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] comprehend and analyze new lifeforms they might encounter, and to make first contact with intelligent beings.<ref name="GG8">''[[Galaxy Guide 8: Scouts]]''</ref> Rin's guide eventually achieved wider distribution. One of the species covered in the work, the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] of [[Dac/Legends|Dac]], deplored the ''Catalog'' for its [[Propaganda/Legends|propagandistic]] claims that Imperial scouts were the first outsiders to make contact with the Mon Calamari species. Rin and his work were infamous on Mon Calamari for [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the publication of his ''Catalog''.<ref name="GATORW" />

===Later research===
{{Quote|Until more research, like the sort undertaken by myself, comes to light, the galaxy will have to continue living in the shroud of ignorance when it comes to these remarkable species.|Obo Rin in a report to Major Vontenn|<ref name="SS" />}}
[[File:MeenaTills-WoSW.png|thumb|left|300px|The Mon Calamari of Dac deplored Rin's work for ignoring their participation in the Old Republic and insisting that the species had instead been first discovered by Imperial scouts.]]
At some point during the later phases of his research, Rin became a permanent member of Darth Vader's personal staff.<ref name="CTD" /> He received a communiqué from Major Vontenn ordering him to compile reports on four amorphous sentient species: the [[Polydroxol]], [[Protean]]s, [[Shi'ido]], and [[Stennes]] Shifters. This addendum to the ''Catalog'', Vontenn explained, was intended to replace information on these species that had been lost from government databanks during the transition from [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] to Galactic Empire.<ref name="SS" />

Over the next four [[Standard month/Legends|standard months]], Rin shifted his focus to these species. He contacted [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Bandor (Moff)|Bandor]],<ref name="SS" /> the Human officer in charge of the planet [[Questal/Legends|Questal]],<ref name="GCQ">''[[The Game Chambers of Questal]]''</ref> for information on the Proteans, only to find that the officer was preoccupied with Rebel Alliance activity on his planet. Nevertheless, Bandor's staff sent Rin Protean medical records, which the sentientologist forwarded to Vontenn. Rin paid [[Slicer/Legends|slicers]] to infiltrate the [[Karflo Corporation/Legends|Karflo Corporation's]] computers to find information about the Polydroxol; he forwarded the slicers' fees to Vontenn as well. Rin's attempts to locate members of the Stennes Shifter species proved fruitless, and the xenobiologist eventually gave up. For his report on the Shi'ido, Rin attempted to contact [[Senior Anthropologist]] [[Vandolae]], a Shi'ido who was employed at the [[University of Coruscant/Legends|University of Coruscant]]. However, Rin received the reply that Vandolae was currently conducting fieldwork on [[Centares/Legends|Centares]], studying the life of [[Fringer/Legends|fringe]] [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]]. Rin traveled to that world to interview the Shi'ido academic, only to discover his colleague dead at the [[Limb/Legends|hands]] of two of his research subjects. Due to these setbacks, Rin contacted Vontenn to request more [[Time/Legends|time]] to do fieldwork beyond the four months he had already taken.<ref name="SS" />

Despite the fall of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] and the splintering of the Empire into rival factions, Rin had become quite famous by the rise of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], and his universal definition of life was still used by scouts.<ref name="GG8" /> His works retained currency, even among officials of the New Republic. [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zgorth'sth]] of the New Republic's [[Department of Threat Assessment]] referenced Rin's entry on the Ailon species, albeit with the caveat that Rin's work had to be understood in the Imperial context in which it was written.<ref name="SMUIU" /> In [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], Rin was still active. After the [[Black Fleet Crisis]], Rin updated his entry on the Yevetha. [[Zeven Mallat]] of the [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] forwarded the file to [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Cinzero Gann|Gann]] of [[Sector 5/Legends|Sector 5]]. The [[New Republic Intelligence Service]] intercepted the transmission and added Rin's work to their dossier of information on the Yevetha and the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]].<ref name="CTD" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|As you can imagine, Lieutenant Pandur, it takes great talent and determination to gather this information. Rest assured that I have made every effort and spared no expense to insure the accuracy of my data.|Obo Rin, "Prologue," ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy''|<ref name="GG4a" />}}
[[File:OrtolansCW.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Rin framed the enslavement of species such as the Ortolans (pictured) as voluntary servitude to the Empire.]]
Obo Rin expressed great satisfaction in his work, relishing the chance to expand the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy's]] base of knowledge on poorly understood and little-documented species.<ref name="SS" /> Rin's writings were primarily drawn from previous research on the species under consideration and his own fieldwork.<ref name="GG4a" /> The scientist relied on a diverse pool of previously compiled research, both published and unpublished, such as classified Imperial experiments and corporate records.<ref name="SS" /> Rin's original field research primarily involved participant observation of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] societies and post-mortem inspections of alien cadavers. He occasionally included hearsay and spacer tales in his work, although he carefully noted when his writings drew from such dubious sources. Once he received his Imperial commission, Rin spared no expense in investigating the species he had selected, paying great sums for specimens to dissect, audiences with powerful beings,<ref name="GG4a " /> fees to slicers to cut into secret databases,<ref name="SS" /> and bribes to access other protected materials. Rin maintained an account at the [[Imperial Bank of Korfo II]], where he received reimbursements for his research costs.<ref name="GG4a" />

Obo Rin presented himself as a die-hard Imperial loyalist, and his work showed a decidedly pro-Imperial bias,<ref name="GG4a" /><ref name="GG4b" /> venturing at times into pure propaganda. For example, his entry on the Mon Calamari species completely dismissed that people's longstanding participation in the Galactic Republic,<ref name="Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith">See, for example, {{Film|III}}.</ref> instead presenting the falsified claim that survey teams from the Galactic Empire made first contact with the planet Mon Calamari and its inhabitants. Rin further claimed that the Empire only established a presence in the species's system to protect them from Rebel Alliance aggression.<ref name="GG4b" /><ref name="GATORW" /> Rin also made claims about the [[Columi/Legends|Columi]] making first contact during the reign of the Empire, despite admitting that they were one of the galaxy's oldest civilizations. The xenobiologist described several species that had been [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] by the Empire—such as the [[Flakax]], [[Jenet/Legends|Jenet]], [[Ortolan/Legends|Ortolans]], [[Squib/Legends|Squibs]], and [[Talz/Legends|Talz]]—as voluntary workers instead.<ref name="GG4b" /> Rin's biases were one source of inspiration for the [[Iyra]] sentientologist [[Tem Eliss]] to write his own work on sentient species, ''[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life]]'', which took a more critical stance on the Empire.<ref name="AEG">{{JournalCite|14|An Extinct Guest}}</ref> Nevertheless, later in his career, Rin also expressed sympathy for species whom he felt were unfairly treated in the galaxy at large.<ref name="SS" /> Rin was something of an egomaniac, believing his theories to be brilliant and holding himself to be a giant in his field.<ref name="GG4a" />

===Theoretical leanings===
{{Quote|…the astute stipulation regarding communication is my own addition to the definition [of sentience]. The particular brilliance of this qualification arises from this: without communication, intelligent beings have no means of organizing themselves into societies. Species which lack societies can cause little inconvenience to the Empire.|Obo Rin, "Prologue," ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy''|<ref name="GG4a" />}}
Obo Rin adhered to a commonly held definition of sentience that defined a sentient species<ref name="GG4a" />—he disdained any academic use of the term ''race''<ref name="GG4b" />—as one with the ability to comprehend abstract concepts. However, he took this a step further,<ref name="GG4a" /> holding that true sentience required not just forming abstract concepts but communicating them as well. The litmus test for this, he averred, was that a species had to be able to discuss a thing's qualities outside the context of the thing itself. By communicating abstractly, Rin believed, species could organize themselves into societies—and species who lacked societies were of little concern to the Empire. Nevertheless, Rin admitted that even trained sentientologists sometimes had difficulty distinguishing non-sentient lifeforms from sentient ones.<ref name="GG4a" />

[[File:Artoo_meets_Wicket.png|thumb|left|300px|Rin's universal definition of life attempted to distinguish non-living beings, such as droids, from living ones.]]
In his work on shape-changing species, the sentientologist eschewed the label ''shape-shifters'' and dismissed the terms ''polymorph'', ''metamorph'', and ''quasimorph'' as misleading since they implied that these species were somehow related to one another.<ref name="SS" /> In Obo Rin's view, a sentient species could not be understood properly without some knowledge of the homeworld on which it had evolved. His ''Catalog'' thus included a great deal of information on various planets that served as the homes of the species detailed, in addition to the information on the species themselves.<ref name="GG4a" />

Rin argued that sentient beings such as [[Droid/Legends|droids]] could be distinguished from living species by the fact that species, as biological entities, exhibited characteristics droids did not. In his ''Catalog'', Rin presented what he called a [[universal definition of life]] to elaborate on this point. The definition stipulated that life entailed organization into some unique morphological structure, irritability to respond to stimuli in the environment, metabolism to provide energy, reproduction to allow the organism to continue beyond a single generation, and adaptation to allow it to adapt and respond to changes in its surroundings.<ref name="GG4a" /> 

As to the components of life, Rin argued that [[Carbon/Legends|carbon]] and [[Water/Legends|water]] were the two most important constituents of living matter, a view that placed him at odds with other scholars, who suggested that [[Silicon/Legends|silicon]] was a reasonable substitute for carbon in creating the building blocks of organic molecules. Rin acknowledged that such silicon-based lifeforms were theoretically possible, but he wrote that he had never encountered one in his travels. Rin also held that [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]] was the third most common component of life, not due to any unique life-giving powers it afforded, but rather because oxygen was one of the constituent components of water; other molecules could theoretically substitute for it.<ref name="GG4a" />

==Behind the scenes==
Author [[Troy Denning]] introduced Obo Rin in ''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'', a book published by [[West End Games]] as part of the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' line in [[1989]]. The book tells of Rin's commission to write the ''Catalog of Sentient Life in the Galaxy'', his patronage by Darth Vader, and his opposition from Major Herrit though a series of communiqués in the introduction. This preface establishes Rin as the [[in-universe]] author of the remainder of the book, which details various alien species for use as characters in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[roleplaying game]].<ref name="GG4a" />

For the release of the [[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races (Second Edition)|book's second edition]] in [[1994]], author [[Chuck Truett]] updated the supplement to be compatible with the [[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition|revised roleplaying rulebook]]. Truett also included new introductory communiqués that establish the reformatted material as a revision by Obo Rin as well, responding to feedback from Darth Vader and Lieutenant Pandur. Obo Rin's revised introduction also hints at an end to the rivalry between himself and Major Herrit, indicating that the latter had died before this revised edition was completed.<ref name="GG4b" />

Later West End Games sources use Rin as the in-universe narrator for information about sentient species. Obo Rin is also mentioned in ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'', published in [[2004]],<ref name="GATORW" /> and in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]].<ref name="CSWE III 101">{{CSWECite|III|101|Rin, Obo}}</ref>
